# terrible_cisco_dnac_network_devices_info_workflow_manager (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `config` (String) List of dictionaries specifying network device query parameters. Each dictionary must contain a `network_devices` list with at least one unique identifier (such as management IP, MAC address, hostname, or serial number) per device.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `config_verify` (Boolean) Set to True to verify the Cisco Catalyst Center after applying the playbook config.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) The desired state of the configuration after module execution.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `response_connected_device_detail` (String) Details of the response containing information about a connected neighbor device.
- `response_device_config` (String) Details of the response containing the device configuration as a string.
- `response_device_info` (String) Device information for network devices, including family, type, software version, serial number, and more. Returned for each device matching the query.
- `response_device_interface_vlan_info` (String) Details of the response containing VLAN information for device interfaces.
- `response_device_interfaces_range` (String) Details of the response containing device interface information retrieved by a specified range.
- `response_device_summary` (String) Details of the response containing a summary of the device.
- `response_interface_info` (String) Details of the response containing interface information for a device.
- `response_inventory_insight_link_mismatch` (String) Details of the response containing device link mismatch information from Inventory Insight API.
- `response_linecard_details` (String) Details of the response containing linecard information for the device.
- `response_module_count` (String) Details of the response containing the count of modules.
- `response_network_device_by_ip` (String) Details of the response containing network device information retrieved by IP address.
- `response_poe_details` (String) Details of the response containing Power over Ethernet (POE) statistics.
- `response_polling_interval` (String) Details of the response containing the polling interval value.
- `response_stack_details` (String) Details of the response containing stack information for the device.
- `response_supervisor_card_detail` (String) Details of the response containing supervisor card information for the device.
